volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: Amphitryon (Section 2.2.726 - Section 2.2.778b)

sections.label: Section 2.2.726
sections.translation: In your sleep , perhaps ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.726b
sections.translation: No — I , awake , saw you awake .

sections.label: Section 2.2.726c
sections.translation: Woe to me !

sections.label: Section 2.2.727
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter with you ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.727b
sections.translation: My wife is mad .

sections.label: Section 2.2.727c
sections.translation: She ’ s attacked with black bile ; nothing so soon turns people mad .

sections.label: Section 2.2.729
sections.original: Vbi primum tibi sensisti , mulier , impliciscier ?
sections.translation: When , madam , did you first find yourself affected ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.730
sections.original: Equidem ecastor sana et salva sum .
sections.translation: Why really , upon my faith , I ’ m well , and in my senses .

sections.label: Section 2.2.730b
sections.translation: Why , then , do you say that you saw me yesterday , whereas we were brought into harbour but last night ? There did I dine , and there did I rest the livelong night on board ship , nor have I set my foot even here into the house , since , with the army , I set out hence against the Teleboan foe , and since we conquered them .

sections.label: Section 2.2.735
sections.original: Immo mecum cenavisti et mecum cubuisti .
sections.translation: On the contrary , you dined with me , and you slept with me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.735b
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.736
sections.translation: I ’ m telling the truth .

sections.label: Section 2.2.736b
sections.original: Non de hac quidem hercle re ; de áliis nescio .
sections.translation: On my honor , not in this matter , really ; about other matters I don ’ t know .

sections.label: Section 2.2.737
sections.translation: At the very break of dawn you went away to your troops .

sections.label: Section 2.2.737b
sections.translation: By what means could I ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.738
sections.original: Recte dicit , ut commeminit : somnium narrat tibi .
sections.translation: She says right , according as she remembers ; she ’ s telling you her dream . But , madam , after you arose , you ought to have sacrificed to Jove , the disposer of prodigies [*] ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.740
sections.original: aut mola salsa hodie aut ture comprecatam oportuit .
sections.translation: either with a salt cake or with frankincense .

sections.label: Section 2.2.741
sections.translation: A mischief on your head !

sections.label: Section 2.2.741b
sections.translation: That ’ s your own business , if you take due care .

sections.label: Section 2.2.742
sections.original: Iterum iám hic in me inclementer dicit , atque id sine malo .
sections.translation: Now again this fellow is talking rudely to me , and that without punishment .

sections.label: Section 2.2.743
sections.original: Tace tu . tu dic : egone abs te abii hinc hodie cum diluculo ?
sections.translation: You hold your tongue . ( To ALCMENA .) Do you tell me now — did I go away hence from you at daybreak ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.744
sections.original: Quis igitur nisi vos narravit mi , illi ut fuerit proelium ?
sections.translation: Who then but your own self recounted to me how the battle went there ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.745
sections.translation: And do you know that as well ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.745b
sections.original: Quippe qui éx te audivi , ut urbem maximam
sections.translation: Why , I heard it from your own self , how you had taken a very large city , and how you yourself had slain king Pterelas .

sections.label: Section 2.2.747
sections.translation: What , did I tell you this ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.747b
sections.original: Tute istic , etiam adstante hoc Sosia .
sections.translation: You yourself , this Sosia standing by as well .

sections.label: Section 2.2.748
sections.original: Audivistin tu me narrare haec hodie ?
sections.translation: Have you heard me telling about this to-day ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.748b
sections.translation: Where should I have heard you ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.749
sections.translation: Ask her .

sections.label: Section 2.2.749b
sections.original: Me quidem praesente numquam factum est , quod sciam .
sections.translation: In my presence , indeed , it never took place , that I know of .

sections.label: Section 2.2.750
sections.translation: It would be a wonder [*] if he didn ’ t contradict you .

sections.label: Section 2.2.750b
sections.translation: Sosia , come here and look at me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.751
sections.translation: I am looking at you .

sections.label: Section 2.2.751b
sections.original: Vera volo loqui te , nolo adsentari mihi .
sections.translation: I wish you to tell the truth , and I don ’ t want you to humour me . Have you heard me this day sav to her these things which she affirms ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.753
sections.original: Quaeso edepol , num tu quoque etiam insanis , quom id me interrogas ,
sections.translation: Prithee now , by my troth , are you , too , mad as well , when you ask me this , me , who , for my part , my own self now behold her in company with you for the first time ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.755
sections.translation: How now , madam ? Do you hear him ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.755b
sections.translation: I do , indeed , and telling an untruth .

sections.label: Section 2.2.756
sections.original: Neque tu illi neque mihi viro ipsi credis ?
sections.translation: Do you believe neither him nor my own self , your husband ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.756b
sections.translation: No ; for this reason it is , because I most readily believe myself , and I am sure that these things took place just as I relate them .

sections.label: Section 2.2.758
sections.translation: Do you say that I came yesterday ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.758b
sections.original: Tun te abiisse hodie hinc negas ?
sections.translation: Do you deny that you went away from here to-day ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.759
sections.original: Nego enim vero , et me advenire nunc primum aio ad te domum .
sections.translation: I really do deny it , and I declare that I have now come home to you for the first time .

sections.label: Section 2.2.760
sections.original: Obsecro , etiamne hoc negabis , te auream pateram mihi
sections.translation: Prithee , will you deny this too , that you to-day made me a present of a golden goblet , with which you said that you had been presented ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.762
sections.original: Neque edepol dedi neque dixi ; verum ita animatus fui
sections.translation: By heavens , I neither gave it nor told you so : but I had so intended , and do so now , to present you with that goblet . But who told you this ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.764b
sections.original: Ego equidem ex te audivi et ex tua accepi manu
sections.translation: Why , I heard it from yourself , and I received the goblet from your own hand .

sections.label: Section 2.2.765b
sections.original: Mane , mane , obsecro te . nimis demiror , Sosia ,
sections.translation: Stay , stay , I entreat you . Sosia , I marvel much how she knows that I was presented there with this golden goblet , unless you have lately met her and told her all this .

sections.label: Section 2.2.768
sections.original: Neque edepol ego dixi neque istam vidi nisi tecum simul .
sections.translation: Upon my faith , I have never told her , nor have I ever beheld her except with yourself .

sections.label: Section 2.2.769
sections.translation: What is the matter [*] with this person ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.769b
sections.translation: Should you like the goblet to be produced ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.769c
sections.translation: I should like it ; to be produced .

sections.label: Section 2.2.770
sections.original: Fiat . heus tu , Thessala , intus pateram proferto foras ,
sections.translation: Be it so . Do you go , Thessala , and bring from indoors the goblet , with which my husband presented me today .

sections.label: Section 2.2.771b
sections.translation: Sosia , do you step this way . Really , I do wonder extremely at this beyond the other wondrous matters , if she has got this goblet .

sections.label: Section 2.2.773b
sections.original: An etiam credis id , quae in hac cistellula
sections.translation: And do you believe it , when it ’ s carried in this casket , sealed with your own seal .

sections.label: Section 2.2.774b
sections.translation: Is the seal whole ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.774c
sections.translation: Examine it .

sections.label: Section 2.2.775
sections.translation: All right , it ’ s just as I sealed it up .

sections.label: Section 2.2.775b
sections.translation: Prithee , why don ’ t you order her to be purified [*] as a frantic person ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.776b
sections.translation: By my troth , somehow there ’ s need for it , for , i ’ faith , she ’ s certainly filled with sprites .

sections.label: Section 2.2.777b
sections.translation: What need is there of talking ? See , here ’ s the goblet ; here it is .

sections.label: Section 2.2.778b
sections.translation: Give it me .
